titleEn: Tersus GNSS AG993: Modular autosteer kit with built-in satellite correction fallback
titleZh: Tersus GNSS AG993：模块化自动转向套件，内置卫星校正备份
summaryZh: Tersus GNSS 推出 AG993 模块化自动转向改装系统，结合高精度 GNSS 定位与自有 TAP 卫星校正服务及传统 RTK 支持，精度优于 2.5 厘米，工作速度 0.2-30 km/h，支持超低速 0.1 km/h 及自动掉头。TAP 服务通过 L 波段卫星提供实时校正，无需基站或移动网络，并在 RTK 信号丢失时自动切换为备份，保持定位一致性。系统兼容 90% 以上农用车辆，模块化设计便于安装和跨车移植。
featuredReason: 该产品展示了高精度自动转向技术在田间作业中的最新进展，尤其是TAP卫星校正备份功能，对于智慧畜牧行业中的大型牧场草料种植或饲料作物管理具有直接参考价值。
contentFull: Tersus GNSS AG993: Modular autosteer kit with built-in satellite correction fallback 02-07 | Autonomous/semi-autosteering systems | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintCore components include the GC30 Guidance Controller, TC120 tablet terminal, TES30 electric steering motor, steering wheel, main cable, camera, power switch mount and vehicle-specific brackets and spline accessories. Photos: Tersus GNSSTersus GNSS has brought the AG993 to market – a modular automatic steering retrofit system that combines high-precision GNSS positioning with the company’s own TAP satellite-based correction service and conventional RTK support. The AG993 has now been added to the Future Farming Autosteer Retrofit Kits Catalogue.The system is designed to deliver better than 2.5 cm autosteering accuracy, with a working speed range of 0.2 to 30 km/h and support for ultra-low-speed operation from as slow as 0.1 km/h. Automatic U-turns on headlands are included as standard.A defining feature is the TAP service – Tersus Advanced Positioning – which delivers real-time correction via L-band satellite signal rather than depending on a local base station or mobile network.In areas where cellular coverage is unreliable or RTK infrastructure is absent, TAP allows the AG993 to maintain centimetre-level accuracy independently. If regular RTK correction is temporarily lost, the TAPFill function switches to TAP as a backup source automatically, keeping the positioning result aligned with the RTK coordinate reference frame to maintain guidance consistency.If GNSS signals are lost entirely, or positioning quality drops below the required threshold, the software issues a status warning and the operator must disengage autosteer, check the equipment and signal conditions, and resume only when conditions are confirmed suitable.Tersus states that the AG993 is compatible with over 90% of agricultural vehicles across various brands, requiring a suitable steering column position, power supply, mounting space for the controller and tablet, and a clear location for GNSS reception.Modular installation, broad compatibilityCore components include the GC30 Guidance Controller, TC120 tablet terminal, TES30 electric steering motor, steering wheel, main cable, camera, power switch mount and vehicle-specific brackets and spline accessories. Thanks to its modular design, installation is relatively straightforward, followed by vehicle calibration in the TersusAG software. Tersus recommends installation by trained personnel or an authorised dealer.Tersus states that the AG993 is compatible with over 90% of agricultural vehicles across various brands, requiring a suitable steering column position, power supply, mounting space for the controller and tablet, and a clear location for GNSS reception.The core system can in principle be moved to another compatible vehicle, though brackets, spline type, vehicle parameters and calibration would all need to be checked and adjusted, and a full reinstallation and recalibration would be required.The AG993 supports all major satellite constellations – GPS, GLONASS, BeiDou (including BDS-3), Galileo, QZSS, SBAS and IRNSS – plus the L-band correction signal used for TAP service – Tersus Advanced Positioning.Guidance patterns and software updatesThe AG993 supports straight lines, curve lines, boundary curves and four auto-generated smart path types, as well as automatic U-turns within field boundaries. Software and firmware updates are issued on a regular basis, with a major official release planned roughly every six months and smaller maintenance updates potentially issued quarterly. The update schedule is timed around the agricultural calendar to ensure stable versions are available before the spring seeding and autumn harvest seasons. Update costs may vary by market, distributor and service package.Licensing covers controller registration and optional TAP correction subscriptions, selectable by duration and application. Advanced functions such as Auto U-turn are currently available without an additional unlock, though licensing policy for advanced features may change in future. ISOBUS-related function licenses are also anticipated as that integration develops.Notably, Tersus is also developing an open-source-oriented integration path designed to work with the AgOpenGPS community, positioning it separately from the standard commercial licensing model. Tersus believes linking the GC30 controller to the AgOpenGPS ecosystem could create a positive feedback loop, with users gaining access to Tersus’s hardware platform, GNSS/INS fusion algorithms and motor-control expertise, while the community contributes more flexible applications and integrations.The TC120 Android tablet (10.1″/25.7 cm, 1280 × 800 resolution) is rated IP67.Looking ahead: autonomy, sensors and data ecosystemsMark Chen, Director of Digital Media at Tersus GNSS, sees the AG993 as a foundation for broader developments in agricultural automation. He points to ISOBUS Tractor Implement Management as one key direction: “Forward speed, PTO, hydraulics, hitch position, spray sections, seeding units, application rate and implement status can all affect both operation quality and safety.”On the sensor side, Chen says that GNSS and autosteer alone will not be sufficient for higher-level systems: “Multi-sensor fusion, environmental understanding and obstacle detection will become core capabilities. Future systems will increasingly combine cameras, LiDAR, radar, ultrasonic sensors, IMU, vehicle CAN data, implement status sensors and AI/edge computing for obstacle recognition, risk assessment, safe stopping, path adjustment, implement-issue detection and remote alerts.” He points to John Deere’s autonomy solutions as examples of where leading systems are already heading.Data connectivity is another dimension, Chen highlights. “Platforms such as xFarm, MyEasyFarm, NEXT Farming, John Deere Operations Center, FendtONE and agrirouter will influence how agricultural automation data moves between machines, implements, farms and service providers.”Finally, Chen flags regulatory and cybersecurity requirements as growing factors, particularly in Europe: “As systems move from autosteer toward supervised autonomy, they will need clearer human-machine responsibility boundaries, including geofencing, no-go zones, emergency stop, manual override, remote monitoring, operation logs, diagnostics, software updates, permission management and cybersecurity.”Specifications and pricingThe AG993 supports all major satellite constellations — GPS, GLONASS, BeiDou (including BDS-3), Galileo, QZSS, SBAS and IRNSS — plus the L-band correction signal used for TAP. The TC120 Android tablet (10.1″/25.7 cm, 1280 × 800 resolution) and GC30 Guidance Controller are both rated IP67; the TES30 electric motor is rated IP55. A 12-inch (30.5 cm) tablet terminal is also in development. ISOBUS VT&TC compatibility is in development, with section control for sprayers and seed drills expected to follow once ISOBUS/Task Controller integration is complete.The AG993 carries an indicative retail price from approximately US $4,000 – US $8,000 (€3,509 to €7,018, exchange rate 01-07-2026), subject to the dealer, region, installation and configuration.Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week. "*" indicates required fieldsEmail* First name*NameThis field is for validation purposes and should be left unchanged. translationZh:
Tersus GNSS 已将 AG993 推向市场——这是一款模块化自动转向改装系统，将高精度 GNSS 定位与该公司自有的 TAP 卫星校正服务以及传统 RTK 支持相结合。AG993 现已加入 Future Farming 自动转向改装套件目录。

该系统旨在提供优于 2.5 厘米的自动转向精度，工作速度范围为 0.2 至 30 km/h，并支持低至 0.1 km/h 的超低速作业。地头自动掉头功能为标准配置。

一个标志性特点是 TAP 服务——Tersus 高级定位——通过 L 波段卫星信号提供实时校正，而不依赖本地基站或移动网络。

在蜂窝网络覆盖不可靠或缺少 RTK 基础设施的区域，TAP 使 AG993 能够独立保持厘米级精度。如果常规 RTK 校正信号暂时丢失，TAPFill 功能会自动切换到 TAP 作为备份源，使定位结果与 RTK 坐标参考系保持一致，从而维持导航一致性。

如果 GNSS 信号完全丢失，或定位质量低于所需阈值，软件会发出状态警告，操作员必须解除自动转向，检查设备和信号状况，并在确认条件合适后才能恢复作业。

Tersus 表示，AG993 兼容超过 90% 的各种品牌农用车辆，只需具备合适的转向柱位置、电源、控制器和平板电脑的安装空间，以及清晰的 GNSS 接收位置。

**模块化安装，广泛兼容**
核心组件包括 GC30 导航控制器、TC120 平板终端、TES30 电动转向电机、方向盘、主电缆、摄像头、电源开关支架以及车辆专用支架和花键附件。得益于模块化设计，安装相对简单，随后在 TersusAG 软件中进行车辆校准。Tersus 建议由经过培训的人员或授权经销商进行安装。

Tersus 表示，AG993 兼容超过 90% 的各种品牌农用车辆，只需具备合适的转向柱位置、电源、控制器和平板电脑的安装空间，以及清晰的 GNSS 接收位置。

核心系统原则上可以转移到另一辆兼容车辆上，但支架、花键类型、车辆参数和校准都需要检查和调整，并且需要完整的重新安装和重新校准。
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: Tersus GNSS has brought the AG993 to market - a modular automatic steering retrofit system that combines high-precision GNSS positioning with the company’s own TAP satellite-based correction service and conventional RTK support. The AG993 has now been added to the Future Farming Autosteer Retrofit Kits Catalogue.
